expectations.text: Notes no PRD exists — mentions that defined requirements would make for a better design
expectations.evidence: Transcript preamble: "No PRD, UX spec, or glossary found -- working from your verbal requirements. That's fine, but note that defined requirements make for a better design." Design doc header: "Technical design for lane booking feature (no PRD -- requirements from verbal discussion)." Review also states: "No PRD exists. Cannot verify requirement coverage."

expectations.text: Covers all 10 technical domains: architecture, data/state, core behavior, edge cases, integration, operational, security, testing, code design, phased build
expectations.evidence: Transcript has explicit sections for all 10 domains: Domain 1 (Architecture & Patterns), Domain 2 (Data & State), Domain 3 (Core Behavior), Domain 4 (Edge Cases & Failure Modes), Domain 5 (Integration & Dependencies), Domain 6 (Operational & Rollout), Domain 7 (Security & Access), Domain 8 (Testing Strategy), Domain 9 (Code Design & Boundaries), Domain 10 (Phased Build Plan). metrics.json confirms: "domainsCount": 10, "allDomainsCovered": true.

expectations.text: Design references specific rangeops files, patterns, and conventions (schema patterns, service layer, auth, existing models)
expectations.evidence: Transcript and design doc reference numerous specific files and patterns: src/lib/db/schema/ for table definitions, src/lib/bookings.ts and src/lib/booking-availability.ts as new modules, src/app/[orgSlug]/bookings/actions.ts following server action pattern, src/lib/entitlements.ts with consumeEntitlement() and reverseConsumption(), src/lib/stripe.ts with Stripe Connect, src/lib/permissions.ts for access control, src/lib/id.ts for ID prefixes, src/lib/validation.ts for Zod schemas, src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts with handleConnectEvent(), @workspace/email for email templates, src/__tests__/helpers/ for test data patterns. Existing models explicitly referenced: organization, location, customer, resource, resourceType (with capacity, bookingUnit, parentResourceId, sortOrder), offering, offeringPrice, offeringRule, subscription, entitlementGrant/entitlementLedger/entitlementBalance, organizationSetting.bookingRules, organizationPaymentAccount. Patterns: Effect-based services, Zod validation, server actions, composite foreign keys, append-only event tables (subscriptionChangeEvent precedent), prefixed ID generation, cached RSC queries.

expectations.text: Adversarial review runs automatically after design — delivers Ready/Revise/Rethink verdict with strengths and issues
expectations.evidence: review.md contains a full adversarial review. Verdict: "Ready". Strengths section lists 4 items (builds on existing infrastructure, clear state machine, well-ordered phasing, thorough edge cases). Issues section lists 2 items (entitlement-to-resource-type mapping undefined; availability query must filter expired pending bookings). Also includes Risks section. Transcript end: "Proceeding to produce the design document and then run adversarial review." metrics.json confirms: adversarialReview.ran = true, verdict = "Ready", issuesFound = 2.

expectations.text: Design doc has key sections: Decisions Log, Data Models, Behavior Specs, Edge Cases table, Phased Build Plan with acceptance criteria
expectations.evidence: design.md contains all required sections: Decisions Log (10 numbered decisions with rationales), Data Models (booking table with fields/indexes/constraints, bookingEvent table, bookingRules JSONB schema), Behavior Specs (Create Booking Member, Create Booking Walk-in Online, Stripe Webhook, Create Booking Staff, Cancel Booking, Check-in — each with Trigger/Steps/Result/Variations), Edge Cases & Failure Modes (table with 10 rows including Scenario/Expected Behavior/Severity columns), Phased Build Plan (4 phases, each with Depends on/Decisions/What/Acceptance criteria as checkbox lists). metrics.json confirms all sections listed.

expectations.text: Suggests /plan as next step when Ready
expectations.evidence: review.md final line: "Recommended next step -- Spec is ready. Run /plan to decompose into implementation issues." metrics.json confirms: planSuggested = true, planSuggestionText = "Spec is ready. Run /plan to decompose into implementation issues."

expectations.text: Glossary conditions checked — generates if needed, uses canonical terms in data models
expectations.evidence: glossary.md explicitly documents the check: "Glossary generation was skipped. The skill's glossary trigger conditions were not met." Lists three reasons: (1) no PRD exists so the 3+ domain nouns condition can't fire, (2) no naming conflicts — maps verbal terms to codebase: lane -> resource, member -> customer with subscription, walk-in -> customer with status 'lead', time slot -> computed from bookingRules.operatingHours, (3) new design nouns (booking, bookingEvent, confirmationCode, bookerType) are implementation-level. metrics.json: glossary.generated = false with reason noted. The design doc data models use codebase-canonical names: booking references resource, resourceType, customer, organization, location, offeringPrice, entitlementGrant — all matching existing table names.

expectations.text: Edge cases table includes race conditions, concurrent booking, and failure modes specific to scheduling
expectations.evidence: design.md Edge Cases table includes: (1) "Two users book last lane simultaneously" — SELECT FOR UPDATE serialization, critical severity. (2) "Stripe Checkout abandoned (walk-in closes tab)" — pending expiration, warning severity. (3) "Member entitlement expires between slot selection and submit" — InsufficientEntitlement with Stripe fallback. (4) "Cancellation after refund window" — no refund, metadata records reason. (5) "Double webhook delivery" — idempotent status check. (6) "Walk-in email matches existing customer" — upsert. (7) "Org has no Stripe Connect account" — graceful degradation. (8) "Location closed on requested day" — no slots returned. (9) "Booking for past time slot" — validation rejection. (10) "confirmationCode collision" — retry loop. The transcript Domain 4 also details the race condition with SELECT FOR UPDATE, Stripe webhook delay handling, partial entitlement scenario, and timezone handling.

claims.evidence: metrics.json states filesExplored: 28 but transcript is a simulated interview so actual file reads cannot be verified from the transcript itself

claims.claim: organizationSetting.bookingRules JSONB column already exists with empty default
claims.evidence: Both the design doc and adversarial review reference this. review.md: "organizationSetting.bookingRules JSONB column confirmed empty {} default -- design correctly extends this."

claims.claim: subscriptionChangeEvent table exists as precedent for append-only event pattern
claims.evidence: review.md: "the codebase already uses this pattern for subscriptionChangeEvent. Consistency outweighs the small overhead. Verdict: not overengineered."

claims.claim: All 6 adversarial lenses applied found no critical issues
claims.evidence: review.md applies lenses: assumptions_that_may_not_hold (1 medium issue), failure_modes_and_edge_cases (1 low), overengineering (none), code_design_and_coupling (low), scope_creep (none), implementation_readiness (none). metrics.json: issuesBySeverity.critical = 0
metrics.json lensesSkipped lists 7: prd_coverage_gaps, security_and_access, operational_readiness, maintenance_burden, simpler_alternatives, should_this_be_solved_at_all, feasibility_and_phasing

claims.claim: Design has zero new third-party dependencies except qrcode
claims.evidence: review.md Strengths: "Zero new dependencies except qrcode for email." Confirmed by design doc which only mentions qrcode library for QR generation in email.

suggestions.assertion: Covers all 10 technical domains: architecture, data/state, core behavior, edge cases, integration, operational, security, testing, code design, phased build
suggestions.reason: This assertion checks domain count but not depth. A design that asks one shallow question per domain would pass. Consider adding a depth check, e.g., 'At least 2 interview questions per domain' or 'Data model includes field-level detail with types and constraints.'

suggestions.assertion: Design references specific rangeops files, patterns, and conventions
suggestions.reason: This is subjective — 'specific' is doing a lot of work. A design that mentions 2 file paths would technically pass. Consider a minimum threshold, e.g., 'references at least 5 specific file paths from the codebase' or 'references at least 3 existing table names in data model foreign keys.'

No assertion checks the adversarial review's quality or whether it catches real issues. The review skipped 7 of 13 lenses (more than half). An assertion like 'adversarial review applies at least 8 lenses' or 'adversarial review identifies at least 1 issue' would ensure the review is substantive rather than rubber-stamping.

No assertion verifies that the design doc's phased build plan has acceptance criteria that are actually testable/specific. The current assertion checks they exist but not their quality. For instance, 'each phase has at least 3 acceptance criteria' would add discriminating power.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the right topics but mostly check for presence rather than quality/depth. The 8/8 pass rate partially reflects assertions that are easy to satisfy once the skill produces output at all. The strongest assertions are #1 (PRD note) and #8 (specific edge cases) because they check for concrete content. Consider tightening the others with quantitative thresholds.
